description: Data-triggered screenshake: on a named event it starts a decaying shake, writing a transient random offset to world.camera.shakeX/shakeY each tick (the renderer adds it), so the view shakes whether or not the scene scrolls. Kept separate from the camera base, so it never disturbs camera-follow or pointer mapping. Emit the event with an optional { magnitude, duration } payload to trigger it as data; a stronger shake refreshes a weaker active one. Deterministic (offset drawn from world.rng). The engine replacement for host-side DOM canvas-translate shake.

event.description: Event name that triggers a shake (default 'shake').

magnitude.description: Default amplitude in px when the payload omits one (default 0).

duration.description: Default length in seconds when the payload omits one (default 0).

falloff.description: Amplitude decay exponent over the duration; 1 = linear, >1 = faster initial settle (default 1).
